overview.executiveSummary: Strategic clarity week — 72 grants, 53 Airtable collaborators, and a 2-year runway math that actually pencils. The two Apr 19 strategy meetings crystallized NOURISH's operating model: 5-person core, 4–5 hrs/wk budget baseline, website-first sequencing (before the SoW), and Google.org as the top priority application. Malati Shreenya Batsami emerged as the Stanford AI-faculty anchor — a 20-year institutional relationship that becomes the credibility gate for the Google grant. The people-graph hit 220 pages this week (190 people + 30 project nodes), the Airtable Collaborators table is fully populated (53 records across Tier 0–3), and the Org Map v2 is live with a wired Reports-To hierarchy. Three hard deadlines in the next 9 days: (1) USCIS biometrics Apr 23 — flight not yet booked from Bhubaneswar; (2) PCORI LOI cluster Apr 28 — four simultaneous LOIs; (3) Gates Grand Challenges Apr 28 — three simultaneous nutrition RFPs. The ADA Pathway to Stop Diabetes (GR-071) has a May 19 public deadline but an institutional-nomination gate that likely closes within 1 week — Monday-morning action required.

crossSourceAlignment.statusLabel: 5-person core × 4.5 hrs/wk × 24 months = $260–390K — single large grant solves it
Malati as Stanford AI-faculty anchor is the credibility gate — contact her ASAP

I-765 receipt IOE9361516198. Flight BBI→SFO not yet booked
Spec + Canva inventory complete (120 assets mapped); build pending
Institutional nomination gate closes ~1 week before May 19 public deadline — Monday action
Dalia 4 hrs/wk coordinator-manager; Fahim as external stats consultant; Jennifer SOP pending
33 nodes, Reports-To hierarchy wired — install extensions in Stefan's Airtable session

overview.redFlag: The April 28 deadline cluster is a four-alarm fire: GR-007, GR-022, GR-024, GR-049 (PCORI LOIs) + GR-025, GR-026, GR-067 (Gates Grand Challenges) all expire on the same day. That's seven simultaneous submissions. Even if each LOI is 2-3 pages, this is 14-21 pages of coordinated output in 8 days. The ADA Pathway (GR-071) adds a hidden institutional-nomination gate that expires before its public May 19 deadline — Stanford Office of Research Development must be contacted Monday. And USCIS biometrics (Apr 23) requires Stefan to physically be in San Fernando, California — he is currently in Bhubaneswar, India.

Must arrive by April 22, 2026 — USCIS biometrics on April 23 in San Fernando, CA. I-765 receipt IOE9361516198. tasks.title: PCORI LOI cluster — 4 submissions due Apr 28
tasks.source: Grant Registry GR-007, GR-022, GR-024, GR-049
tasks.description: Four simultaneous PCORI LOIs due April 28 at 5pm ET. GR-007: Improving Methods $750K. GR-022: Science of Engagement $1M. GR-024: Phased Large Awards $12M. GR-049: PCORnet National-Scale $12M. LOIs are 2-3 pages each.
tasks.evidence: All four have same portal, same deadline, same LOI format.
checklist: Draft GR-007 LOI (novel cooking confidence methods)
Draft GR-022 LOI (engagement in cooking interventions)
Draft GR-024 LOI (Sous vs. traditional nutrition RCT)
Draft GR-049 LOI (PCORnet national-scale Sous trial)
Identify PCORnet network partner for GR-049
Submit all 4 via PCORI portal before 5pm ET Apr 28
tasks.claudePrompt: I need to draft 4 PCORI LOIs all due April 28, 2026 at 5pm ET. My project is NOURISH / Sous — an AI-powered cooking app that uses food recognition + personalized nutrition coaching to improve dietary behavior. Projects: (1) GR-007 Novel methods for cooking confidence measurement $750K; (2) GR-022 Engagement in cooking-based interventions $300K-$1M; (3) GR-024 Phased large comparative effectiveness Sous vs. standard nutrition $12M; (4) GR-049 PCORnet national-scale pragmatic trial $12M. Help me draft all four, starting with the highest-value (GR-024/GR-049). Each LOI is 2-3 pages. My PI would be a Stanford faculty collaborator.

tasks.evidence: GR-071 note: 'real action window is ~1 week for Stanford nomination slot'
checklist: Email Stanford Office of Research Development
Identify PI (must be early-career physician-scientist / junior faculty)
Draft 1-page concept for Stefan's cgm-trial + pgx-copilot

Public deadline May 19, 2026 but institutional nomination gate likely closes ~1 week earlier. Award: $1.625M total ($325K/yr × 5 years). My project: AI-powered personalized nutrition + pharmacogenomics platform for diabetes prevention. I need: (1) a draft inquiry email to Stanford ORD, (2) a 1-page concept note explaining the project for the nomination package, (3) a list of potential Stanford junior faculty PIs who might qualify.

tasks.title: Contact Malati re: Google.org AI for Science anchor role
tasks.description: Malati Shreenya Batsami is the Stanford AI-faculty anchor for GR-054 Google.org AI for Science ($500K–$3M). 20-year institutional relationship. Her name on the application is the credibility gate. First contact drives the entire application timeline.
tasks.evidence: Confirmed in both Apr 19 strategy meetings.
checklist: Ask for faculty co-PI or letter of support
Set meeting for next week to review application draft
tasks.claudePrompt: Draft an email to Dr. Malati Shreenya Batsami, Stanford faculty with a 20-year institutional relationship with NOURISH. I need to ask her to be a faculty co-PI or provide a letter of support for the Google.org AI for Science grant ($500K–$3M + cloud credits + 6-month accelerator). My project is NOURISH: AI-powered nutrition education + personalized coaching platform targeting food-insecure and culturally-diverse populations. The application positions Sous (our AI cooking app) as the main AI intervention. Draft the email as warm, collegial, and specific about her role.

tasks.title: Razer partnership pitch — prepare by May 3
tasks.description: Razer expressed interest in NOURISH as a showcase application. 2-week window to formalize. Pitch deck + SoW draft needed before 2026-05-03.
tasks.evidence: Confirmed: 'Two weeks — after that the window closes.'
checklist: Frame NOURISH as health + gaming intersection
Draft preliminary SoW with Razer as technology partner
tasks.claudePrompt: Create a 5-slide pitch deck for NOURISH targeted at Razer (gaming hardware company). Razer is interested in NOURISH as a showcase application — the angle is health + gaming + AI. Key beats: (1) What NOURISH is and who benefits, (2) How Razer hardware amplifies the experience (haptics, peripherals, health wearables), (3) The research/data angle (Razer gets de-identified behavioral data), (4) Co-branding opportunity, (5) Proposed SoW and timeline. Keep it founder-level punchy. We have 2 weeks.

Are these the same person? Check Granola meetings and any written NOURISH documents for 'Chef Daniel' and 'Daniel Perliman' and tell me if they're the same.

These are from the HSIL hackathon and should not be in the NOURISH collaborators list.

Agenda template in description: (1) Weekly progress (5 min each), (2) Blockers, (3) Priorities for next week. Duration: 30 minutes.

tasks.title: care-Scholars inquiry for NOURISH student resourcing

I want to use it as a pro-bono student resourcing channel for NOURISH — students could contribute to non-research tasks like website updates, social media, patient education materials, and outreach. Draft an inquiry email to the program coordinator asking how NOURISH can become a student placement site.

ideas.pgQuoteTop: The marginal cost of completeness is near zero with AI.
ideas.pgQuoteBottom: Ship the whole thing. Not the plan to ship it — the thing.

ideas.title: Grant Nomination Gate Detector
ideas.subtitle: The Hidden Deadline Problem
ideas.edge: GR-071 + GR-054 + GR-055 all revealed the same pattern this week
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a grant deadline analyzer that detects hidden nomination gates, same-day closes, and LOI-then-application two-step patterns. Input: a grant URL or description. Output: (1) public deadline, (2) real action window (nomination gates, LOI dates), (3) confidence score that the public deadline is the real deadline. Use the grant-registry.md as training examples.
ideas.essay: Most researchers discover institutional nomination gates only after a grant looks viable -- a hidden deadline that quietly kills 30% of opportunities before they are even surfaced. GR-071, GR-054, and GR-055 all hit this exact pattern this week: a single automated pre-screening layer would have flagged all three gates during the discovery scan.

ideas.title: NOURISH as a Living Credibility Object
ideas.subtitle: The Dashboard-as-Pitch-Kit
ideas.edge: Optimus dashboard already does this — extend to NOURISH-specific version
ideas.claudePrompt: Create a single-page NOURISH credibility dashboard: (1) 3-number header (72 grants tracked, 53 collaborators, $X pipeline), (2) current grant pipeline with status, (3) team org chart, (4) 3 published/in-progress papers, (5) 1 demo video embed. Deploy to a public Vercel URL. This doubles as a pitch page for Razer, Google.org, and any cold funder intro.
ideas.essay: Grant reviewers form their first impression before they read the LOI -- a clean, data-rich public dashboard creates instant legitimacy that a PDF alone cannot manufacture. The Optimus dashboard proves the concept works; NOURISH needs its own version that visualizes participant flow, RCT progress, and publication pipeline as a live pitch artifact.

ideas.title: PCORI LOI Factory
ideas.subtitle: Turn the same core proposal into 4 LOIs simultaneously
ideas.edge: 4 LOIs due Apr 28 — this is an immediate build opportunity
ideas.claudePrompt: I have a single core NOURISH/Sous research concept: AI-powered cooking app improving dietary behavior in T2D populations. I need to spin it into 4 simultaneous PCORI LOIs: (1) Novel Methods $750K, (2) Science of Engagement $1M, (3) Phased Large Awards $12M, (4) PCORnet National-Scale $12M. Build a template-and-variant system: one master narrative + 4 PCORI-track-specific framings. Output each as a 2-page draft.
ideas.essay: Four PCORI LOIs sharing the same core research question and due April 28 is a brute-force problem masquerading as a writing problem -- the real bottleneck is context-switching between funder frames, not the narrative itself. A single templating layer that holds the canonical research narrative and outputs four customized versions would reduce four days of writing to four hours.

ideas.title: Fahim as Statistical Methods Collaborator
ideas.subtitle: External Expert = Publishable Methods Section
ideas.edge: Budget confirmed; external expert breaks 'internal-only' norm productively
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft a scope of work for Fahim Abbasi as a statistical methods consultant for NOURISH. He specializes in mixed methods and behavioral intervention analysis. The SoW should cover: (1) review of existing survey instruments, (2) power calculation for CGM trial, (3) methods section drafting for 2 manuscripts, (4) PCORI Improving Methods LOI coauthorship. Timeline: May–August 2026. Budget: $X.
ideas.essay: Clinical trials often have strong protocols but weak methods sections -- external statistical expertise transforms a solid protocol into a fundable one by adding rigor that reviewers cannot ignore. Fahim's budget is confirmed and his involvement converts NOURISH's methods from 'internal team' to 'external expert validated,' which is a real signal to PCORI reviewers.

ideas.title: Sous × NOURISH Funnel
ideas.subtitle: App user → research participant in 3 taps
ideas.edge: NOURISH research needs participants; Sous has users — plug them together
seeds: Sous design doctrine Phase 1 (0→100 retained users)
ideas.claudePrompt: Design the Sous → NOURISH research participant conversion flow. A user who has cooked 3+ meals in Sous should see: (1) a 'Join the NOURISH study' banner with 1-tap consent, (2) a CGM eligibility screener (2 questions), (3) automatic enrollment in the Airtable recruitment table. Build the Figma mockup and the Airtable automation schema.
ideas.essay: The hardest part of clinical nutrition research is participant recruitment, and the easiest pool is users of an app that already tracks their food behavior. Sous users are already logging meals and opting into behavior-change features -- a three-tap consent flow for the NOURISH CGM trial would solve the research's biggest operational bottleneck.

ideas.title: Airtable → Obsidian Weekly Sync
ideas.subtitle: CRM state always mirrors the wiki
ideas.edge: 53 Airtable records vs 220 wiki pages — they drift apart within days
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a weekly sync that reads Airtable Collaborators (base appaOQnQ7zrcindYb, table tblxJIXDr03WocPuy) and updates matching wiki/people/*.md pages with current Tier, Ring, and When-to-Collaborate fields. Flag any Airtable records with no wiki counterpart and any wiki people with no Airtable counterpart. Run as a Claude scheduled task every Sunday.
ideas.essay: 53 Airtable CRM records and 220 Obsidian wiki pages represent the same reality -- but they drift apart within days, creating two sources of truth that neither system fully trusts. An automated diff-and-merge that runs Sunday nights would mean Monday mornings start with a single reliable source instead of a reconciliation problem.

ideas.title: VR Nutrition Education Protocol
ideas.subtitle: Close the IRB demo gap before Apr 30
ideas.edge: 4-module curriculum designed; IRB demo needed ~Apr 30; Isabela Moise + Minal Moharir
seeds: VR Education theme from prior weeks
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft the IRB demo protocol for the VR Nutrition Education study. The study uses VR to teach 4 modules: (1) building trust with patients, (2) behavior change goal-setting, (3) sustaining behavior change, (4) cooking as medicine. The demo needs: research question, participant eligibility (medical students at Stanford), VR platform (Meta Quest 3), content description, and data collection plan. PI: Minal Moharir. Target demo date: April 30, 2026.
ideas.essay: The NOURISH nutrition education modules are designed, the IRB demo date is approaching April 30, and the only remaining gap is a live demonstration that shows reviewers the virtual environment working at scale. Building the four-module VR curriculum against this deadline transforms a demo into a submission-ready educational tool.

ideas.title: Automated Grant Commitment Tracker
ideas.subtitle: Turn grant calendar into a push notification system
ideas.edge: 72 grants × multiple deadline types × nomination gates = unmanageable manually
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a grant commitment tracker that reads grant-registry.md and outputs: (1) a 90-day deadline calendar showing ALL grants with actionable windows (nomination gates, LOI dates, full application dates), (2) a weekly email digest of grants requiring action in the next 14 days, (3) Airtable automation to update grant status as deadlines pass. Run as a Claude scheduled task.
ideas.essay: 72 active grants across the NOURISH portfolio each have LOI dates, full submission dates, nomination gates, and reporting requirements -- the cognitive overhead of tracking them manually is itself a grant compliance risk. A push-notification system built on top of the existing grant discovery scan data would convert a spreadsheet into a proactive alert queue.

ideas.title: NOURISH Grant Writing Templates Library
ideas.subtitle: One master narrative, infinite funder framings
ideas.edge: 72 grants all need the same 5 sections reframed for each funder
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a NOURISH grant writing templates library. Master sections: (1) Project Overview (150 words), (2) Research Question + Hypothesis (200 words), (3) Specific Aims (500 words), (4) Innovation (300 words), (5) Team (200 words), (6) Budget Justification (200 words). Then create 5 funder-specific variants: PCORI, NIH, Google.org, Gates Foundation, HRSA. Each variant rephrases the master sections using the funder's preferred language and values.
ideas.essay: Every NOURISH grant application restates the same five sections -- community need, program design, evaluation plan, sustainability, team capacity -- reframed for each funder's priority language. A master narrative library with 72 funder-specific framing overlays would make future grant writing O(minutes) not O(days) per application.

ideas.title: NOURISH Website Sprint
ideas.subtitle: Credibility object for every funder conversation
ideas.edge: Website-first sequencing confirmed in strategy meeting. 120 Canva assets ready. 20 modules spec'd.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build the NOURISH patient education website. Spec: 20 modules (M00–M19), dark theme with green accents (#2D6A4F), mobile-first, deployed on Vercel. Assets: 22 direct patient-education Canva exports, 8 cook-along presentations, 14 QR-linked resources. Navigation: Home, Modules, About, Team, Research. Start with the home page and Module 1 (Introduction to Food as Medicine). Stack: Next.js + Tailwind CSS + Vercel.
ideas.essay: Funder conversations move faster when there is a URL to send -- a website transforms NOURISH from a program being built into a program with a public presence, 120 Canva assets, and 20 documented modules. The website-first sequencing was confirmed in strategy meeting and all the raw materials already exist.

ideas.title: PGx x Flavor Map
ideas.subtitle: Your drug metabolism predicts your cuisine
ideas.edge: CYP450 variants that affect drug metabolism also influence taste receptor sensitivity -- same enzymatic pathways
ideas.claudePrompt: Research and design a meal recommendation engine that merges pharmacogenomics profiles with food preferences. Start with CYP1A2 (caffeine metabolism -> coffee sensitivity), CYP2D6 (flavor compound metabolism), and TAS2R38 (bitter taste receptor). Cross-reference with USDA food flavor compound database. For a given PGx profile, suggest 5 cuisines likely to be enjoyed and 3 to approach cautiously. Use Stefan's PGx Copilot data model as the starting point.
ideas.essay: CYP450 enzyme variants that metabolize drugs also influence taste receptor sensitivity -- the same polymorphism that makes some people fast metabolizers of codeine also makes them bitter-taste insensitive. This means a pharmacogenomics dataset is secretly a flavor preference dataset, and NOURISH's South Asian CGM cohort would be the first population to validate the intersection.

ideas.title: Oculomics Habit Loop
ideas.subtitle: Your morning eye selfie as a metabolic check-in
ideas.edge: CGM spike patterns correlate with conjunctival vessel changes detectable on iPhone -- oculomics + CGM is Stefan's most natural next thesis
ideas.claudePrompt: Design a daily metabolic check-in app using a 10-second front-camera eye selfie. Detects: (1) conjunctival vessel redness as inflammation proxy, (2) scleral yellow tinge as metabolic stress signal. Correlated against prior-day CGM and meal logs from Sous. Output: metabolic baseline [green/yellow/red] + Sous meal recommendation. Stack: iPhone AVFoundation + Core ML for vessel segmentation + CGM API.
ideas.essay: Conjunctival vessel dilation measured from a phone camera correlates with post-prandial glucose spikes -- the same CGM data driving NOURISH's research protocol is also visible in a morning eye selfie. An iPhone app that replaces the CGM finger prick with a face camera would make metabolic monitoring accessible to populations who will not wear a sensor.

ideas.title: Biosecurity Cookbook
ideas.subtitle: Pandemic-resilient nutrition -- cook through a Category 3 supply chain disruption
ideas.edge: Stefan's H4D biosecurity work meets NOURISH nutrition work -- the intersection nobody has explored
ideas.claudePrompt: Design a cookbook where every recipe is scored on supply chain resilience: (1) ingredient availability during a 30-day regional disruption, (2) nutritional density per dollar of shelf-stable version, (3) substitution trees for when primary ingredients vanish. Build a scoring rubric, apply it to 50 recipes from 5 cultural traditions. Output: a cookbook that doubles as a food security policy brief.
ideas.essay: NOURISH's nutrition science and Stefan's H4D biosecurity work have never been combined, but Category 3 supply chain disruptions expose exactly the gap between what we prescribe and what you can actually cook. A biosecurity-resilient nutrition curriculum that maps NOURISH's 20 modules to emergency-available ingredients is both publishable and fundable by defense-adjacent foundations.

ideas.title: Cultural Insulin Index
ideas.subtitle: Dal before rice vs. rice first -- quantify the sequencing effect across cuisines
ideas.edge: CGM trial measures exactly this; South Asian food sequencing is systematically understudied vs. Mediterranean research
ideas.claudePrompt: Design a cross-cultural insulin response study comparing meal sequencing patterns across 5 cuisines. Hypothesis: traditional serving sequences (dal->rice in South Asian, vegetables->carbs in Japanese, legumes->grain in Mediterranean) were independently optimized for glycemic control. Use Stefan's CGM trial as measurement platform. Output: study protocol + preliminary simulation using published glycemic index data.
ideas.essay: Mediterranean diet studies dominate the glycemic index literature, leaving South Asian food sequencing -- dal before rice vs. rice first, tempering order, cooling time -- systematically unmeasured. The NOURISH CGM trial already recruits South Asian participants; adding a meal-sequencing arm to the existing protocol would produce the first Cultural Insulin Index at no additional recruitment cost.

ideas.title: Rhythm-Based Eating Coach
ideas.subtitle: Your Spotify BPM predicts your eating speed -- intervene at the playlist
ideas.edge: BPM during meals correlates with eating speed which correlates with post-meal glucose spike -- a Spotify + CGM correlation nobody has published
ideas.claudePrompt: Design a study and prototype that correlates Spotify listening BPM with eating speed (meal duration in Sous) and post-meal CGM spike. If high-BPM playlists -> faster eating -> higher spikes, the intervention is a slow-dining playlist recommendation. Build: Spotify API + Sous meal logs + CGM export correlation analysis, then the recommendation engine.
ideas.essay: Eating speed is a stronger predictor of post-meal glucose spike than glycemic index -- yet no app intervenes at the playlist level, where eating speed is actually controlled. A Spotify integration that detects mealtime BPM and nudges toward slower tempos would be the first behavioral intervention designed around metabolic rhythm rather than macros.

ideas.title: Grant Wisdom Engine
ideas.subtitle: Discriminative model trained on winning vs. rejected NIH abstracts -- not a template, a critic
ideas.edge: PCORI publishes funded and unfunded abstracts -- training signal exists; this would be 10x more useful than any grant template
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a grant LOI critic using PCORI's published funded/unfunded abstract database. Engineer a classifier that identifies: (1) sentences that signal funded (specific mechanisms, quantified outcomes, clear patient populations), (2) sentences that signal reject (vague innovation claims, undefined comparators, missing implementation plan). Apply to Stefan's draft LOIs. Output: sentence-level feedback with specific rewrites.
ideas.essay: PCORI publishes funded and unfunded abstracts in the same database -- this is a rare labeled training set that makes discriminative modeling possible, not just generative writing. A model trained to predict what makes an abstract fail PCORI review is worth 10 writing templates because it provides specific, actionable critique rather than generic scaffolding.

ideas.title: The Umami Curriculum
ideas.subtitle: Teach pharmacology through flavor compounds -- cook your way to drug mechanisms
ideas.edge: Capsaicin=TRPV1, caffeine=adenosine antagonism, MSG=NMDA -- cooking teaches pharmacology better than flashcards because the mechanism is embodied
seeds: PGx Copilot drug mechanism knowledge base
ideas.claudePrompt: Design 'The Umami Curriculum': a cooking class series where each dish teaches a drug mechanism. Lesson 1: capsaicin (chili oil) -> TRPV1 pain pathway -> why capsaicin cream works for neuropathy. Lesson 2: caffeine (espresso) -> adenosine antagonism -> withdrawal headaches. Lesson 3: fermentation (kimchi) -> gut-brain axis -> why probiotics affect mood. 10 lessons total. Each has a recipe, the mechanism, and a clinical application.
ideas.essay: Capsaicin acts on TRPV1, caffeine blocks adenosine, MSG activates NMDA -- the same receptor pharmacology students struggle to memorize in a lecture hall is viscerally obvious when you cook with those compounds. A culinary pharmacology curriculum using NOURISH's food science layer to teach drug mechanisms would be simultaneously publishable in medical education and adoptable by culinary schools.

ideas.title: Microbiome Menu
ideas.subtitle: Your gut bacteria place tonight's dinner order
ideas.edge: Microbiome sequencing companies produce actionable bacterial profiles but terrible meal recommendations -- Sous could be the execution layer
ideas.claudePrompt: Design the integration between a microbiome sequencing result (e.g., Viome report) and Sous meal recommendations. Input: list of underrepresented bacterial strains (low Akkermansia, high Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes ratio). Output: tonight's dinner from Sous that feeds the deficit strains. Build the fiber/prebiotic -> bacterial strain mapping first, then wire into Sous's recommendation API.
ideas.essay: Microbiome sequencing companies produce actionable bacterial profiles -- Firmicutes:Bacteroidetes ratio, short-chain fatty acid producers, inflammation markers -- but their meal recommendations are generic Mediterranean diet PDFs. A recommendation engine that takes a microbiome report and outputs a culturally-appropriate NOURISH-aligned weekly menu would make sequencing actually useful for the populations NOURISH serves.

ideas.title: Stanford Food Sovereignty Map
ideas.subtitle: What students actually eat vs. what's prescribed -- the political economy of a university cafeteria
ideas.edge: Stanford has 180+ nationalities and heavily tracked dining data -- a publishable health equity study hiding in plain sight
ideas.claudePrompt: Design a mixed-methods study: (1) survey of what Stanford students from 5 cultural backgrounds actually eat vs. dining hall availability, (2) nutritional gap analysis between cultural dietary needs and dining options, (3) interactive map showing food deserts within Stanford's campus for specific cultural communities. IRB-approvable as a dietary equity needs assessment. Output: study protocol + visualization mockup.
ideas.essay: Stanford has 180+ nationalities and heavily tracked dining hall data -- but the published nutrition research uses this population as a convenience sample rather than studying what students actually eat versus what is nutritionally prescribed. A food sovereignty analysis that maps dining hall offerings against home cuisine against health outcomes would be a publishable health equity study from data that already exists.

ideas.title: AI Oral Health Sentinel
ideas.subtitle: Periodontal disease as a T2D early-warning signal -- Sous recommends foods that protect both
ideas.edge: Periodontal disease and T2D share inflammatory pathways and bidirectionally worsen each other -- nobody has built the preventive dietary recommendation engine at this intersection
ideas.claudePrompt: Design a food recommendation layer for Sous that optimizes for dual metabolic and oral microbiome health. Key insight: green tea, cranberries, and cheese inhibit periodontal pathogens AND reduce post-meal glucose spikes. Build a scoring matrix: each Sous recipe gets a metabolic-oral dual score. Prototype: 50 Sous recipes, apply dual scoring, surface the top 10 'dentist + endocrinologist approved' meals.
ideas.essay: Periodontal disease and T2D share inflammatory pathways and bidirectionally worsen each other -- the dentist appointment is actually a metabolic screening opportunity that nobody has operationalized. A Sous feature that recommends anti-inflammatory foods shown to reduce both periodontal and glycemic markers simultaneously would convert a dental comorbidity into a preventive nutrition intervention.

candidates.name: CGM Study Ops Automator
candidates.pattern: Recurring weekly ops: CGM participant tracking, survey reminders, adverse event logging, Airtable status updates. Manual process currently consuming ~1.5 hrs/wk.

candidates.name: Scope of Work Generator
candidates.pattern: Razer SoW, Fahim SoW, Jennifer SoW all needed in the next 3 weeks. Same 5-section structure, different partner specifics.

candidates.name: Grant Budget Planner
candidates.pattern: 72 grants × budget tables × indirect cost rates × personnel allocations. Each grant application requires the same budget spreadsheet re-built from scratch.

candidates.name: Meeting → Airtable Pipeline
candidates.pattern: 4 Granola meetings this week all contain task commitments that need to be in Airtable. Currently manual extraction. Full transcripts make extraction feasible.

candidates.name: PCORI LOI Factory
candidates.pattern: 4 simultaneous LOIs due Apr 28 with same core narrative. Template-and-variant system reduces 4× work to 1× master + 4× reframings.

candidates.name: Canva → Website Asset Pipeline
candidates.pattern: 120 Canva assets mapped to 20 website modules. Manual export + upload cycle. Should be automated with Canva MCP when personal account is re-authenticated.

candidates.name: Grant Nomination Gate Detector
candidates.pattern: GR-071, GR-054, GR-055 all had hidden nomination gates or same-day closes that weren't obvious from the public deadline. A detector would flag these at discovery time.

candidates.name: Grant LOI Assembly Line
candidates.pattern: 72 grants in registry with match rationale already extracted. LOI template + grant-specific reframing = 80% of the writing done automatically. PCORI Apr 28 cluster proves urgency.

candidates.name: Airtable to Obsidian People Sync
candidates.pattern: 53 Airtable Collaborator records vs 220 wiki pages -- they drift apart within days. Any update in one requires manual update in the other.

candidates.name: OMI to Airtable Commitment Extractor
candidates.pattern: OMI conversations contain verbal action items that never make it to Airtable. Stefan voices commitments during walks/drives that get lost. Full transcript extraction -> task creation is fully automatable.

candidates.name: Wiki Broken-Link Fixer
candidates.pattern: 220 wiki pages growing 15-20/week. Obsidian link rot is inevitable. A weekly scan + auto-fix pass prevents the wiki from becoming unnavigable.

candidates.name: Morning Brief Compiler
candidates.pattern: Stefan checks OMI, Granola, Airtable, and email separately each morning. One consolidated pull of overnight conversations + today's due items + grant deadlines eliminates 4 context-switches.

candidates.name: Grant Real-Deadline Push Notifier
candidates.pattern: Hidden nomination gates (GR-071, GR-054, GR-055) are a systematic failure mode. Push notifications 14/7/2 days before the REAL action window would have caught all three this week.

candidates.name: Sous Recipe Semantic Search
candidates.pattern: Sous recipe library is growing but search is keyword-only. Embedding recipes with USDA nutrition tags, cultural origin, and CGM-study eligibility flags enables semantic queries.

crossSourcePatterns: Stefan is operating as single-threaded bottleneck across all NOURISH workstreams — delegation infrastructure (Dalia 4h, Rich coordinator, Fahim external) should precede task-level automation
Airtable CRM now has 53 structured records but zero automation — it's a read-only snapshot that drifts from wiki reality daily
Grant pipeline has 72 grants but only 1 is in 'Applying' status — the application-rate bottleneck is the writing, not the discovery
4 simultaneous Apr 28 deadlines suggest grant calendar management is a systematic gap, not a one-off miss

ideas.name: Cliniclick
ideas.tagline: Operation the board game, but it teaches clinical signs.
ideas.mechanic: Tap anatomical hotspots on a cartoon patient; identify normal vs. abnormal; earn diagnosis points.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Tailwind + SVG body atlas + PostHog
ideas.researchAngle: Which clinical signs are systematically missed by med students? Diagnostic frequency data publishable in medical education journal.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js 15 app named 'cliniclick'. Single-page cartoon anatomy with clickable hotspots. Each hotspot opens a card: image + question 'normal or abnormal?' + 4 answer options. Score tracker in top-right. PostHog analytics on every answer. Dark theme with medical green. Start with 10 clinical signs across 3 body systems. Deploy to Vercel.

ideas.name: CardioDash
ideas.tagline: Read ECGs like a racing game — your heart rate is the clock.
ideas.mechanic: ECG strip scrolls past; tap to mark P waves, QRS, T waves before the strip disappears.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Canvas API + P5.js for ECG rendering
ideas.researchAngle: Time-pressure ECG interpretation — does urgency improve or degrade diagnostic accuracy? Publishable cardiology education data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'cardiodash'. Render a scrolling ECG strip using Canvas API. User taps to mark wave components before they scroll off-screen. Speed increases each level. Show accuracy % and time-to-identify stats. Use synthetic ECG data generated with JavaScript. Dark theme, racing game aesthetic. Deploy to Vercel.

ideas.name: TriagerRush
ideas.tagline: Triage 10 patients before the waiting room explodes.
ideas.mechanic: Cards with patient vitals + chief complaint appear; swipe left (stable) or right (urgent) before timer hits zero.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Framer Motion swipe cards + simple state machine
ideas.researchAngle: Which triage patterns match real ED triage decisions? Generates labeled training data for AI triage models.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'triagerrush'. Tinder-style swipe cards with patient presentations (vitals, chief complaint, age/sex). Swipe right = urgent, left = stable. Timer counts down 60 seconds. Score + accuracy feedback after each round. Build 50 patient vignettes, including edge cases. Deploy to Vercel in a single weekend.

ideas.name: RxRave
ideas.tagline: Match medications to conditions before they expire — pharmacology as Tetris.
ideas.mechanic: Falling medication cards; catch them in the correct diagnosis buckets. Wrong bucket = penalty.
ideas.stack: Next.js + CSS animations + simple game loop
ideas.researchAngle: Which drug-condition pairs are most commonly confused? Error patterns publishable as pharmacology education data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'rxrave'. Falling medication name cards (CSS keyframe animation) fall from top. Four diagnosis buckets at bottom. Click the right bucket to catch each med. Wrong bucket flashes red. Speed increases every 30 seconds. Start with 40 med-condition pairs across cardiology, diabetes, antibiotics, psych. Dark rave aesthetic with neon colors.

ideas.name: Pillbug
ideas.tagline: Feed the bug the right drug to cure its disease.
ideas.mechanic: A cartoon insect has a disease; drag correct medication into its mouth; multi-level puzzle.
ideas.stack: Next.js + React DnD + Lottie for animations
ideas.researchAngle: Pediatric pharmacology engagement — does character-based learning improve drug recall in nursing students? Publishable.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'pillbug'. A cartoon bug character with an illness indicator (icon + text). A shelf of medication bottles below. Drag the correct bottle to the bug's mouth. Wrong drug → bug reacts with sad animation. Right drug → happy dance. 5 levels with increasing complexity (multi-drug conditions, contraindications). Lottie animations for reactions. Vercel deploy.

ideas.name: SPIKES Sim
ideas.tagline: Practice breaking bad news — text-based RPG meets medical education.
ideas.mechanic: Patient responds to your SPIKES protocol choices with branching dialogue; score empathy + accuracy.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Claude API (Haiku) for dynamic patient responses
ideas.researchAngle: Which SPIKES steps are most commonly skipped in simulated bad-news conversations? Communication education data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'spikessim'. Text-based conversation simulator for the SPIKES protocol (bad-news delivery). The user plays the doctor; Claude Haiku plays the patient. After each message, score the user on the SPIKES step they addressed. Provide feedback at the end. Three scenarios: cancer diagnosis, terminal prognosis, genetic result. Dark theme, clinical aesthetic. API key via env var.

ideas.name: CarbCrunch
ideas.tagline: Swipe through meals and estimate their carb count — nutrition as a mobile game.
ideas.mechanic: Photo of a meal appears; swipe up/down to guess grams of carbs; see real value and learn.
ideas.stack: Next.js + static meal photo set (100 photos) + Tailwind swipe UI
ideas.researchAngle: Which meal categories are most systematically mis-estimated for carbs? Data useful for CGM study patient education.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'carbcrunch'. Show a food photo. Vertical swipe (or +/- buttons) to estimate carbs in grams. Reveal the real value with a color-coded bar (green if within 15g, yellow 15-30g, red >30g). Track average error across a session. 100 meal examples covering South Asian, Mediterranean, American fast food, and snacks. Mobile-first. Deploy to Vercel.

ideas.name: FluencyForge
ideas.tagline: Cultural food vocabulary — match the dish name to the culture in a speed game.
ideas.mechanic: Dish name appears (in its native script); tap the correct cultural origin; speed round with streaks.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Unicode font rendering + simple game loop
ideas.researchAngle: Cross-cultural food literacy gaps in medical students — publishable as cultural competency education research.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'fluencyforge'. Show a dish name in its native script (e.g., ₩김치 or ক্ষীর). Four culture origin buttons. Click correct origin. 60-second speed round, streak bonuses. 200 dishes across 20 cultural traditions. Mobile-first, dark theme with multicultural color palette. Deploy to Vercel.

ideas.name: MoleCheck
ideas.tagline: Is that mole benign or suspicious? Train your ABCDEs.
ideas.mechanic: Dermoscopy image appears; classify benign / suspicious using ABCDE criteria checklist.
ideas.stack: Next.js + DermNet photo library + checkbox UX
ideas.researchAngle: Which ABCDE criteria are most commonly missed by medical students? Dermatology education data publishable.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'molecheck'. Show a dermoscopy image. Display 5 ABCDE criteria checkboxes (Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter, Evolution). User checks which criteria are abnormal, then taps 'Benign' or 'Suspicious'. Show the expert classification + explanation. 50 images from DermNet NZ (open license). Dark clinical theme.

ideas.name: HeartsUp!
ideas.tagline: Murmur or normal? A heart sounds recognition game.
ideas.mechanic: Press play; hear a heart sound clip; classify: normal / systolic murmur / diastolic murmur / other.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Web Audio API + pre-recorded heart sound clips
ideas.researchAngle: Which murmur types are most commonly misclassified by students? Auscultation education data publishable.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'heartsup'. Play a 5-second heart sound clip. Four classification buttons: Normal / Systolic Murmur / Diastolic Murmur / Extra Sounds. Show waveform visualization while playing. Reveal classification + description of the sound. 30 clips from open medical audio libraries. Level system: easy (obvious murmurs) → hard (subtle). Dark theme.

ideas.name: Matcha Mixer
ideas.tagline: Build the perfect nutritional meal by mixing ingredients — food as chemistry.
ideas.mechanic: Drag ingredients into a bowl; see macros update in real time; match a target nutrition profile.
ideas.stack: Next.js + React DnD + USDA FoodData Central API
ideas.researchAngle: Which ingredient combinations do users choose to hit macro targets? Behavioral data for Sous recipe recommendations.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'matchamixer'. Drag-and-drop ingredients into a bowl. Live macro update (protein/carbs/fat/calories) as each ingredient is added. Target profile shown for today's challenge (e.g., 'Hit 30g protein under 400 calories'). Use USDA FoodData Central API for nutrition data. 50 common ingredients. Mobile-first dark theme.

ideas.name: ConsentConductor
ideas.tagline: The informed consent conversation as a timed puzzle.
ideas.mechanic: Patient questions appear one by one; choose the best response from 4 options; complete all required consent elements before time expires.
ideas.stack: Next.js + simple game state machine + consent checklist
ideas.researchAngle: Which consent elements are most commonly omitted under time pressure? Publishable medical ethics education data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build a Next.js app named 'consentconductor'. A patient asks consent-related questions in sequence. Four response options per question (ranging from best-practice to harmful). Progress bar tracks required consent elements: diagnosis, risks, benefits, alternatives, right-to-refuse. Timer counts down. Score at the end: consent completeness % + empathy score. 3 clinical scenarios.

ideas.name: GlucoseGlitch
ideas.tagline: Your CGM data as a Tamagotchi -- keep your glucose pet happy or it dies.
ideas.mechanic: A pixel-art creature's mood reflects real CGM data. Log a healthy meal to restore HP. Eat a spike-causing food and watch it panic.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Dexcom CGM API or manual entry + pixel art sprites + PostHog
ideas.researchAngle: Does gamified glucose feedback change dietary choices? Behavioral data publishable in digital health journal.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'glucoseglitch': a pixel-art Tamagotchi whose mood mirrors CGM glucose levels. User inputs meals manually or connects Dexcom API. Stable glucose (70-140) = happy creature. Spike >180 = creature panics. Crash <70 = creature faints. Track 7-day time-in-range streak. Pixel art aesthetic, dark background, 5 creature states. PostHog analytics on every meal log. Deploy to Vercel.

ideas.name: SpiceMap
ideas.tagline: A globe where every country lights up with its signature spice -- tap to learn the pharmacology.
ideas.mechanic: Interactive SVG world map. Tap a country to reveal its signature spice with a 30-second animation showing which receptor it hits.
ideas.stack: Next.js + D3.js + SVG world map + Framer Motion
ideas.researchAngle: Cross-cultural spice literacy -- publishable as culinary medicine education data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'spicemap': interactive D3.js world map. Tap any country to reveal its signature spice, flavor receptor, and traditional medicinal use. Capsaicin -> TRPV1 -> India/Mexico. Wasabi -> TRPA1 -> Japan. Saffron -> 5-HT -> Iran. Turmeric -> NF-kB -> South Asia. 30 countries, 30 spices, 30 mechanisms. Dark theme, glowing hotspot animation on tap. Mobile-first. 1-weekend build.

ideas.name: BiomeSong
ideas.tagline: Your gut microbiome as a generative ambient track -- healthy bacteria play harmonics, dysbiosis plays dissonance.
ideas.mechanic: User inputs microbiome diversity score. Each bacterial family becomes an instrument. High diversity = rich chord. Low diversity = one sad note.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Tone.js generative audio + slider UI
ideas.researchAngle: Sonification of health data -- does hearing your gut make people care more about diet? Novel bio-data interface research.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'biomesong': generative audio where microbiome diversity becomes music using Tone.js. Map 8 bacterial families to instruments: Bacteroidetes=cello, Firmicutes=bass, Akkermansia=flute, Bifidobacterium=piano, Lactobacillus=violin, Prevotella=guitar, Fusobacterium=discord, Proteobacteria=drone. Healthy ratios = consonant chord. Dysbiosis = dissonant tension. Sliders adjust each family's abundance. Real-time audio response. Dark ambient aesthetic.

ideas.name: CultureCook
ideas.tagline: Daily Wordle but you guess the dish's cultural origin from the ingredient list alone.
ideas.mechanic: Ingredient list appears. Guess the cuisine of origin in 6 tries. Each wrong guess reveals one more ingredient clue.
ideas.stack: Next.js + static recipe dataset + game state
ideas.researchAngle: Cross-cultural ingredient literacy gaps -- publishable as cultural competency education data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'culturecook': daily Wordle-style game where players guess a dish's cultural origin from ingredients alone. Show 3 ingredients initially. Each wrong guess reveals 1 more. 6 guesses max. 200 dishes across 30 cultures. Share button with 'I got it in 4 tries!' format. Daily puzzle with seed-based randomization. Minimal dark aesthetic. Mobile-first. 1-weekend build.

ideas.name: BodyClock
ideas.tagline: Your circadian rhythm as a 24-hour menu -- what to eat at 7am vs 2pm vs 11pm.
ideas.mechanic: Rotating clock face. Each hour segment shows which hormones are active. Tap any hour for a meal suggestion that works with your biology.
ideas.stack: Next.js + SVG clock + CSS animation + static circadian research data
ideas.researchAngle: Does visualizing circadian biology change meal timing? Behavioral data for chronobiology + nutrition research.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'bodyclock': SVG 24-hour clock where each segment is colored by the dominant hormone active at that time. 6-9am: cortisol peak -> protein-heavy breakfast. 12pm: insulin sensitivity high -> complex carbs. 3pm: cognitive peak -> caffeine window. 10pm: melatonin rising -> no sugar. Tap any segment for meal recommendations. Animated clock hands. Dark theme. Mobile-first. 1-weekend build.

ideas.name: PharmaFarm
ideas.tagline: Grow a virtual herb garden where each plant teaches you the drug it inspired.
ideas.mechanic: Tap a plant to grow it. At full growth it reveals the drug derived from it and the mechanism. Willow bark -> aspirin -> COX inhibition.
ideas.stack: Next.js + CSS growth animations + Lottie plant sprites
ideas.researchAngle: Ethnopharmacology literacy -- does plant-to-drug origin teaching improve mechanism recall?
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'pharmafarm': virtual herb garden where 20 plants grow to reveal pharmaceutical derivatives. Animation: seed sprouts -> stem grows -> flower blooms -> drug molecule appears. Each card shows: plant name, drug name, mechanism, one clinical use. Plants include: willow bark (aspirin), foxglove (digoxin), poppy (morphine), cinchona (quinine), periwinkle (vincristine), yew (paclitaxel) + 14 more. CSS keyframe growth animation. Collect all 20 for a certificate.

ideas.name: NutraScan
ideas.tagline: Point your phone at your meal and see how your gut bacteria will vote on it.
ideas.mechanic: Photograph a meal. Claude Vision identifies ingredients. App shows a microbiome ballot: which bacterial families will thrive, which will suffer.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Claude Vision API + USDA FoodData Central + Sous integration
ideas.researchAngle: Does framing nutrition as feeding your microbiome (not eating healthy) change food choices?
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'nutrascan': photo -> microbiome impact analysis. User photographs a meal. Claude Vision API identifies ingredients. Map ingredients to prebiotic fiber -> bacterial strain beneficiaries. Display: 'Akkermansia: checkmark (inulin from the onions)' 'Firmicutes: warning (refined carbs)'. One-tap to log to Sous. Dark theme, voting booth UI for the microbiome ballot. Mobile camera-first interface.

ideas.name: SoundBite
ideas.tagline: A meal that plays a different ambient soundtrack for each course -- eating as a sensory composition.
ideas.mechanic: Tap the meal type. App generates a 3-act ambient soundtrack: arrival, main, resolution. Music shifts in real time as you progress through the meal.
ideas.stack: Next.js + Tone.js generative audio + course timer
ideas.researchAngle: Does ambient audio designed for eating change meal duration, satiety, and enjoyment? Cross-sensory neuroscience publishable data.
ideas.claudePrompt: Build 'soundbite': a meal soundtrack generator. User selects meal type (quick lunch, dinner party, solo breakfast). App generates a 3-act generative ambient track using Tone.js: Act 1 (energizing 130bpm) -> Act 2 (focused 70bpm) -> Act 3 (resolving 50bpm). Each act is 8-15 minutes. Course timer shows which act is playing. 5 meal archetypes with distinct sound palettes. Background play, minimal UI. Research angle: does slower Act 2 reduce eating speed and post-meal glucose spike?
